error_codes.meaning: Water supply not detected
error_codes.detail: Your Samsung washer cannot sense adequate water pressure from the supply lines. The machine detects insufficient water flow when attempting to fill. This is one of the most common Samsung washer errors and is usually caused by a simple supply issue rather than a hardware failure.

causes.description: Water supply valve partially or fully closed
Kinked, pinched, or clogged inlet hose
Low water pressure from home supply (below 20 psi)
Faulty water inlet valve not opening fully

fixes.title: Check the water supply valves
fixes.description: Locate the hot and cold water supply valves behind the washer. Turn both fully counterclockwise (open). Even a slightly closed valve can trigger a 4C error.

fixes.title: Inspect the inlet hoses
fixes.description: Pull the washer out and check both inlet hoses for kinks or bends. Straighten any kinks. Also check the mesh screens inside the hose connections — clean them if clogged with sediment.

fixes.title: Check home water pressure
fixes.description: Run a tap nearby. If pressure is low throughout the home, the issue is your supply, not the washer. Low pressure (below 20 psi) will trigger 4C.

fixes.title: Test and replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If all above checks pass, the water inlet valve solenoid may be stuck. Test continuity with a multimeter. Replace if no continuity reading.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before accessing the inlet valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn the washer off and back on. If the water supply issue is fixed, the code should clear. Alternatively, press and hold the Start/Pause button for 5 seconds to reset mid-cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a technician if you have confirmed water supply is good (pressure OK, valves open, hoses clear) but the error persists — the control board or water inlet valve needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load detected
error_codes.detail: The washer cannot spin safely because the laundry load is unevenly distributed in the drum. This is a safety feature — an unbalanced drum at spin speed can damage the bearings, drum suspension, and surrounding cabinetry. The washer will pause and attempt to redistribute the load before displaying UE.

causes.description: Single heavy item or small mixed load bunched to one side
Overloaded drum (too many items packed tightly)
Underloaded drum (one or two items only)
Worn drum suspension springs or shock absorbers

fixes.title: Redistribute the laundry
fixes.description: Open the door and manually redistribute items evenly around the drum. Separate any tangled items like sheets or towels that may have clumped together.

fixes.title: Check the load size
fixes.description: Remove some items if the drum is overloaded (more than ¾ full). Add a few items if washing 1–2 small items alone — small loads imbalance easily.

fixes.title: Run a spin-only cycle
fixes.description: Select Spin Only at the lowest speed and let it complete. If the cycle finishes without UE, the load is now balanced.

fixes.title: Inspect suspension rods (persistent UE only)
fixes.description: If UE occurs consistently with normal loads, inspect the 4 suspension rods. Shake the drum by hand — it should resist movement and spring back. If it flops freely, replace the suspension rods.

error_codes.reset_instructions: UE clears automatically once the drum is balanced. The washer may make several attempts to rebalance before displaying the error. No manual reset needed — just open and redistribute the load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UE occurs with every load regardless of size or balance, the drum suspension springs or shock absorbers are likely worn and need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Samsung washer cannot drain water within the expected time. This is a high-priority error as it will prevent the cycle from completing and leave standing water in the drum. The most common cause is a clogged pump filter, which is a 5-minute DIY fix.

fixes.title: Clean the pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the filter access panel on the lower-front of the washer. Place a towel and shallow pan below it. Turn the cap counterclockwise slowly — water will drain out. Remove and clean the filter under running water. Check for coins, pins, or lint buildup.
fixes.warning: Expect a significant amount of water. Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Inspect the drain hose at the back for kinks. Disconnect it from the standpipe and blow air through it to check for blockages.

fixes.title: Check the household standpipe
fixes.description: Make sure the drain standpipe is not clogged. Run water down it directly. If it backs up, the issue is your home plumbing, not the washer.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If the above steps are clear, the pump motor may have failed. You can hear the pump when the washer tries to drain — a grinding or no-sound indicates failure. Replace the drain pump.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, restart the washer. If water remains in the drum, run a Spin/Drain-only cycle. The error should clear once a successful drain cycle completes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a pro if the drain pump has been replaced and the error persists — there may be a control board or wiring harness issue.

error_codes.meaning: Motor locked — drum cannot rotate
error_codes.detail: The washer motor has stalled or is drawing excessive current, preventing the drum from spinning. This can be caused by an overloaded drum, a foreign object jamming the motor, or a failed motor or hall sensor. This is a high-severity error that will stop the cycle.

causes.description: Drum overloaded with too much laundry
Foreign object (bra wire, sock) jammed between drum and tub
Worn or failed motor brushes (older models)

fixes.title: Reduce the load
fixes.description: Turn off the washer. Remove several items from the drum and try restarting. LE from overloading is very common.

fixes.title: Check for foreign objects
fixes.description: Slowly rotate the drum by hand. If it feels stuck, remove all laundry and check around the drum gasket seal for items caught in the space between drum and door seal.

fixes.title: Reset the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart. If the motor stalled from a momentary overload, this may clear it.

fixes.title: Inspect motor brushes (top-load models)
fixes.description: On older top-load Samsung models, the motor brushes wear down and cause stalling. If you hear the motor trying to start but failing, brushes are likely worn.
fixes.warning: Requires accessing the motor — unplug first.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor or motor
fixes.description: If drum rotation is physically free but the error persists, test the hall sensor with a multimeter. If resistance is out of spec, replace it. As a last resort, the motor itself may need replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5–10 minutes, remove excess laundry, then plug back in and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the drum is free, load is normal, and the error persists after power cycling, the motor or control board needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Door not properly closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The washer detected that the door is not securely latched, or the door lock mechanism has failed. For safety, the washer will not run with an unlocked door. This is often a simple latch alignment issue but can indicate a failing door lock assembly.

causes.description: Door not fully pushed closed (laundry caught in seal)
Damaged or worn door latch/strike plate
Wiring harness to door lock loose or damaged

fixes.title: Check for items caught in the door seal
fixes.description: Pull back the rubber door gasket and check for socks or small items caught between the gasket and drum. A small item can prevent the door from fully closing.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Push the door firmly until you hear and feel a click. The lock is spring-loaded and requires a definitive push.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch and catch
fixes.description: Look at the door catch on the door and the strike inside the machine. If the plastic is cracked or the latch feels loose, replace the assembly.

fixes.title: Test the door lock solenoid
fixes.description: If the latch looks fine but the error persists, the lock solenoid (the electrical component inside the door lock) may have failed. Test with a multimeter for continuity.
fixes.warning: Unplug before testing the solenoid.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Make sure the door is fully closed and click-locked. The error should clear when the cycle restarts with a properly secured door.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes and clicks but the error still shows, the door lock solenoid or wiring needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water heater error — water not heating to target temperature
error_codes.detail: The washer is not able to heat the wash water to the programmed temperature within the expected time. This affects wash quality for hot water cycles and can indicate a failing heater element or thermistor.

causes.description: Heater relay failure on control board
Wiring fault between heater and control board

fixes.title: Check water supply temperature
fixes.description: Make sure the hot water supply to the washer is actually hot. Run the hot water tap at the sink — if it's cold, the issue is your water heater.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor (NTC sensor)
fixes.description: The thermistor monitors water temperature. Test resistance at room temperature — should be approximately 10k ohms. If significantly out of spec, replace it.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test the heating element with a multimeter for continuity. No continuity means the element has burned out.
fixes.warning: The element is inside the drum tub — requires significant disassembly.

fixes.title: Inspect the control board heater relay
fixes.description: If element and thermistor both test good, the heater relay on the control board may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes and restart. If the error is intermittent and clears on restart, the thermistor is failing. If it returns consistently, the heater element likely needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating element replacement requires disassembling significant parts of the washer. If you're not comfortable with appliance repair, call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected
error_codes.detail: Too much detergent has been used, creating excess foam in the drum. The washer will pause and wait for the suds to dissipate before continuing. This is not a mechanical failure — it is a safety pause.

causes.description: Wrong detergent type — non-HE detergent in HE washer
Detergent residue buildup from previous overuse

fixes.title: Wait for suds to settle
fixes.description: The washer will automatically continue once suds reduce enough. This may take 15–30 minutes. Do not turn off the washer.

fixes.title: Run a Rinse+Spin cycle
fixes.description: If the cycle doesn't continue within 30 minutes, run a Rinse+Spin cycle to clear the suds.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent going forward
fixes.description: For HE (High Efficiency) washers, use HE-labeled detergent only, and use significantly less than the measuring line suggests — 1–2 tablespoons for a normal load.

fixes.title: Clean the drum (if recurrent)
fixes.description: Run a drum clean cycle monthly to remove detergent residue buildup.

error_codes.reset_instructions: SUD clears automatically once foam reduces. No manual reset needed. If you want to speed up the process, run a Rinse+Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is not a fault requiring professional service. If it occurs every cycle despite using minimal HE detergent, the detergent dispenser drawer may need cleaning.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor (thermistor) fault
error_codes.detail: The temperature sensor in the washer has failed or is reading an implausible temperature. The washer cannot safely heat water without an accurate temperature reading, so it stops the cycle.

causes.description: Wiring harness connector loose at thermistor
Short circuit in temperature sensor wiring

fixes.title: Check thermistor wiring connector
fixes.description: Locate the thermistor (usually mounted on the heater assembly inside the tub). Check that the wiring connector is firmly seated.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer first.

fixes.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixes.description: At room temperature (~68°F/20°C), a healthy Samsung thermistor should read approximately 6,000–7,000 ohms. A reading of 0 or infinite indicates failure.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Thermistors are inexpensive and easy to replace. Disconnect the connector, unscrew the sensor, and install the new one.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor or reseating the connector, unplug the washer for 1 minute, then restart to clear the error.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor tests good and connector is secure but the error persists, the main control board may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Button error — key stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: One or more buttons on the control panel are registering as continuously pressed or shorted. This can be caused by a stuck button, moisture in the panel, or a failing user interface board.

causes.description: Button physically stuck from detergent residue
Moisture or condensation in control panel

fixes.title: Check for stuck buttons
fixes.description: Press each button individually and ensure none feel stuck. Clean around buttons with a dry cloth.

fixes.title: Dry the control panel
fixes.description: If moisture has entered the panel, dry the area thoroughly. Leave the machine off for several hours.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5–10 minutes. This often clears transient button errors.

fixes.title: Replace the UI board
fixes.description: If the error persists, the user interface board needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes. If a button is stuck, clear it before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no button appears stuck and the error returns after a power cycle, the control board needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor (temperature sensor) failure
error_codes.detail: The dryer's temperature sensor has failed or is reading out of range. The dryer cannot safely heat the drum without knowing the current temperature, so it stops the cycle.

fixes.title: Locate and test the thermistor
fixes.description: The thermistor is mounted on the heating element housing. Test resistance with a multimeter — should read ~10k ohms at room temperature.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Unplug and inspect the thermistor connector for corrosion or loose fit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: This is a simple clip-in repair. Disconnect the old sensor and plug in the new one.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before any repair.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor, unplug for 1 minute then restart. The code clears when a successful cycle completes without temperature anomaly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the thermistor is new and tests correctly, the control board's temperature input circuit may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Heater fault — drum not heating or overheating
error_codes.detail: The dryer heating element is not functioning correctly. This can mean it's not producing heat at all, or it's reaching unsafe temperatures. Clothes will come out damp or the dryer will stop mid-cycle.

causes.description: Tripped high-limit thermostat from restricted airflow
Faulty heater relay on control board

fixes.title: Check the lint filter and vent
fixes.description: A completely blocked vent can cause the high-limit thermostat to trip, cutting off heat. Clean the lint filter and disconnect/clean the vent duct.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect the element wires and test continuity. A working element will show resistance (around 10–20 ohms); an open element shows infinite resistance.

fixes.title: Test the high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: If the element is good but heat isn't reaching the drum, the high-limit thermostat may have tripped permanently. Test for continuity — no continuity = failed.

fixes.title: Replace the failed component
fixes.description: Replace whichever component tested as failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug dryer and ensure it's gas-disconnected if applicable before opening.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, the code may require clearing by unplugging for 5 minutes. Check that vent is clear before first test run.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both the element and thermostat test OK, the issue is likely on the control board and requires professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Vent blockage detected
error_codes.detail: The dryer's internal sensors have detected restricted airflow through the exhaust duct. Continuing to run with a blocked vent is a fire hazard — the dryer will reduce heat or stop the cycle.

causes.description: Lint-blocked exhaust duct (most common after 1+ years)
Kinked or crushed flexible duct behind the dryer
Bird or pest nest in exterior vent cap
Duct run is too long or has too many bends

fixes.title: Clean the lint filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the lint screen. This should be done after every single drying cycle.

fixes.title: Disconnect and clean the vent duct
fixes.description: Pull the dryer out, disconnect the duct from the back, and blow through it. If you feel resistance, clean with a dryer vent brush kit.

fixes.title: Check the exterior vent cap
fixes.description: Go outside and find where the dryer vents. Remove any debris, lint buildup, or animal nesting blocking the flap.

fixes.title: Inspect duct routing
fixes.description: Flexible foil or plastic ducts kink easily. Replace with rigid metal duct for runs longer than 4 feet. Maximum recommended run is 25 feet with fewer than two 90° turns.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a short air-dry cycle. The sensor clears once airflow is detected as normal.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct is completely clear and code persists, the airflow sensor or control board may need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Leak sensor activated — water detected at base of dishwasher
error_codes.detail: The leak sensor in the base pan of the dishwasher has detected standing water, indicating a leak somewhere in the unit. The dishwasher will not run until the water is removed and the source of the leak is addressed. This is one of the most searched Samsung dishwasher errors.

causes.description: Overloaded detergent causing excessive suds and foam overflow
Loose or failed door seal allowing water to leak during cycle
Cracked or loose water inlet hose connection
Drain hose loose at pump connection
Wash arm cracked or broken, spraying water sideways

fixes.title: Remove water from the base pan
fixes.description: Tilt the dishwasher slightly forward (remove the bottom rack first) to drain accumulated water from the base pan. Use towels to soak up remaining water.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before tilting the unit.

fixes.title: Check detergent usage
fixes.description: If you used too much detergent or the wrong type (non-HE), this creates excess foam that overflows into the base. Use only dishwasher-specific detergent, maximum 1 tablespoon per cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect the door gasket
fixes.description: Run your hand along the entire door seal. Look for tears, hardened sections, or sections that have pulled away from the channel. A bad seal lets water out during the cycle.

fixes.title: Check all hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect the water supply line, inlet valve connection, drain hose at the pump, and drain hose at the sink drain. Tighten any loose fittings. Replace cracked hoses.

fixes.title: Inspect the wash arms
fixes.description: Remove the lower wash arm and check for cracks. A cracked arm sprays water at pressure against the base, triggering the sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After removing water from the base pan and fixing the leak source, the sensor should reset automatically once dried. Run the dishwasher with no detergent on a short cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the leak source is not obvious and the LC code persists, the leak detection float switch itself may be stuck. A technician will be able to locate the exact leak path.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining from dishwasher
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher cannot drain within the expected time. Water will remain standing in the bottom of the tub. This usually means the drain pump is blocked or the drain hose is kinked.

causes.description: Debris blocking the drain filter and sump area
Blocked sink drain or garbage disposal knockout plug not removed

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack. Unscrew the cylindrical drain filter (turn counterclockwise), lift out the flat filter underneath, and rinse both under running water. This should be cleaned monthly.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Trace the drain hose from the back of the dishwasher to the sink drain/garbage disposal. Make sure it has a high loop (up to the underside of the counter) and is not kinked.

fixes.title: Check garbage disposal
fixes.description: If connected to a garbage disposal, run the disposal before starting the dishwasher. Also confirm the knockout plug was removed when the disposal was installed.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear, the pump may have failed. You should hear it running when the dishwasher attempts to drain. No sound = possible pump failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a rinse cycle. The code clears once a complete drain cycle succeeds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a pro if the drain filter and hose are clean but the pump is not running — the pump motor or wiring needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Wash motor error — circulation pump failure
error_codes.detail: The main circulation pump (the motor that sprays water during the wash cycle) has failed or is drawing incorrect current. The dishwasher cannot run a wash cycle without a functioning circulation pump.

causes.description: Foreign object (broken glass, bone) jammed in pump impeller

fixes.title: Check for debris in the pump
fixes.description: Remove the filter assembly and reach into the sump area. Feel around the pump impeller for any hard debris. Remove carefully.
fixes.warning: Sharp broken glass may be present — use gloves.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixes.description: Disconnect power for 5 minutes and restart. If the pump jammed briefly, a reset may clear it.

fixes.title: Test the pump motor
fixes.description: Access the pump from under the dishwasher. Test the motor windings with a multimeter. No continuity indicates a burned motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing any jams, unplug for 1 minute then restart. Pump replacement requires significant disassembly and the error will clear after a successful wash cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement is a complex repair best left to a technician if you're not experienced with appliance repair.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker fan motor error or ice room fan failure
error_codes.detail: The fan motor in the ice maker compartment or ice room has failed or is running at incorrect speed. This prevents proper cold air circulation to the ice maker, resulting in no ice production and potential temperature rises in the freezer section.

causes.description: Fan motor failure (bearing failure or burned windings)
Ice or frost buildup blocking the fan blade
Fan blade physically broken or stuck
Wiring harness to fan motor damaged

fixes.title: Defrost the ice maker area
fixes.description: Turn the refrigerator to demo/off mode and let the freezer section warm to room temperature. Ice buildup around the fan is extremely common and will jam the blade without breaking it.

fixes.title: Inspect the fan blade
fixes.description: Locate the ice maker fan (usually behind the ice maker assembly). Spin the blade by hand — it should rotate freely. If stuck, clear any ice or debris.

fixes.title: Test the fan motor
fixes.description: Disconnect the fan and apply 12V DC directly (from a phone charger). If the motor doesn't spin, it has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: Remove the ice maker assembly to access the fan. Swap the motor assembly.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before disassembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, press and hold both the 'Energy Saver' and 'Power Freeze' buttons simultaneously for 5 seconds to clear diagnostic error codes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan and ice are clear but the motor doesn't spin with direct voltage, proceed with replacement. If the new motor also shows errors, the control board input is faulty.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker fan motor error
error_codes.detail: The fan motor responsible for circulating cold air through the ice maker compartment is not operating correctly. Without this fan, the ice maker cannot maintain freezing temperatures and ice production stops.

causes.description: Fan wire harness disconnected or damaged

fixes.title: Defrost the freezer section
fixes.description: Turn off the refrigerator and let all ice melt. This resolves fan blade blockage.

fixes.title: Check wiring to ice maker fan
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the fan motor and at the control board.

fixes.title: Replace ice maker fan motor
fixes.description: If motor doesn't spin freely or shows no continuity, replace it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold 'Energy Saver' + 'Power Freeze' for 5 seconds to enter diagnostic mode and clear the error after repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice buildup is recurring (freezer OK but ice maker section keeps frosting over), the defrost heater in that zone may be failing.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator sensor error — freezer temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The temperature sensor on the evaporator coils in the freezer has failed or is reading incorrectly. The control board uses this sensor to manage defrost cycles. A faulty sensor can lead to excessive frost buildup or insufficient defrost.

fixes.title: Locate the evaporator sensor
fixes.description: The evaporator sensor is clipped to the evaporator coils behind the back panel in the freezer compartment.

fixes.title: Test the sensor resistance
fixes.description: At 32°F/0°C, a healthy Samsung thermistor reads approximately 16.3k ohms. Test and compare to spec.

fixes.title: Check and reseat connector
fixes.description: The connector often vibrates loose over time. Unplug and firmly reseat.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: The thermistor clips onto the evaporator coil. Unclip the old one, clip in the new one, reconnect.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug for 5 minutes and plug back in. Run a manual defrost cycle by holding 'Freezer Temp' and 'Lighting' for 8 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor is new and correct but errors continue, the main PCB sensor input circuit may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer defrost sensor error
error_codes.detail: The defrost sensor that monitors the freezer during defrost cycles has failed. Without accurate temperature readings during defrost, the control board cannot safely end the defrost cycle, risking food damage from overheating or frost accumulation from underheating.

causes.description: Connector loose at sensor or control board
Sensor shorted from ice or moisture

fixes.title: Locate the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Behind the freezer back panel, near the defrost heater. Often a two-wire sensor clipped to the evaporator.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature: should read approximately 5.5k–6.5k ohms. At 32°F: ~16k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect, unclip from evaporator, and install new sensor. Handle the evaporator carefully.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after repair. The code should clear after a successful defrost cycle (every 6–12 hours normally).
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Recurring defrost errors with correctly functioning sensors point to a defrost heater or timer issue requiring professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Display test mode active — not a fault code
error_codes.detail: Your Samsung refrigerator is in display test mode or showroom demo mode. This is not a malfunction. The compressor and cooling system are disabled. All 8s on the display confirm this mode.

causes.description: Display board failure causing erratic readout

fixes.title: Exit demo mode
fixes.description: The method depends on your model. Most commonly: hold 'Energy Saver' and 'Power Freeze' simultaneously for 5 seconds. Or: hold 'Freezer Temp' and 'Refrigerator Temp' for 5 seconds.

fixes.title: Check your manual
fixes.description: If the above combination doesn't work, check your specific model's manual for the demo mode exit sequence.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Exit demo mode as described above. The display should return to normal temperature readings.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you cannot exit demo mode using standard button combinations, the display board may have a software fault. Contact Samsung support.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker function error — ice maker not cycling
error_codes.detail: The ice maker is detecting a fault in its operating cycle. The ice maker motor or sensor has not responded within the expected time. Ice production has stopped.

causes.description: Ice maker frozen solid — ice stuck in tray or ejection arm
Water inlet solenoid to ice maker not opening

fixes.title: Force a manual defrost
fixes.description: Hold the test/reset button on the ice maker for 3 seconds. If the ice maker is frozen, the motor strain will reveal it.

fixes.title: Defrost the ice maker
fixes.description: Turn off the refrigerator and place towels in the freezer. Let the ice maker thaw completely (2–4 hours). Check that the ejection arm moves freely.

fixes.title: Test the ice maker water supply
fixes.description: Listen for the water fill valve clicking when a new cycle starts. If you hear clicking but no water fills, the inlet solenoid to the ice maker is faulty.

fixes.title: Replace ice maker assembly
fixes.description: If defrost and water supply are fine but the motor still won't cycle, replace the ice maker module.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press the reset button on the ice maker. Alternatively, turn the ice maker off and back on using the ice maker switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the ice maker is replaced and errors continue, the freezer is not cold enough (below 0°F/-18°C required) or the control board is not sending power to the ice maker.

error_codes.meaning: Drainage error — water not draining within expected time
error_codes.detail: The LG washer is unable to drain water within the programmed time. This will result in standing water in the drum and a halted cycle. The pump filter is usually the culprit — it's a 10-minute DIY fix that most people overlook.

causes.description: Clogged pump filter (lint, coins, small items)

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: The pump filter is behind the lower-front panel. Place towels and a shallow bowl below it. Turn the cap slowly counterclockwise — water will drain out. Remove the filter, clean it, and check for obstructions in the housing.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — expect a significant amount of water.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: The drain hose runs from the back of the washer to the standpipe. Make sure it's not kinked, and the hose height is between 28–47 inches from the floor.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Pour water directly into the standpipe. If it backs up, the blockage is in your plumbing.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear, run a drain cycle and listen. If you hear nothing (no motor sound), the pump has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the pump filter or hose blockage, run a Spin Only cycle. The OE code clears once a successful drain cycle completes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the pump filter is clean and hose is clear but no draining occurs and the pump is silent, the pump motor needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load — cannot complete spin cycle
error_codes.detail: The washer's sensors detected that the laundry load is unevenly distributed, making it unsafe to run the spin cycle at high speed. LG washers will make several attempts to redistribute the load by adding water and slowly tumbling before displaying UE.

causes.description: Heavy items clumped to one side of the drum
Single large item (comforter, towels) that absorbs water unevenly
Worn suspension springs or shock absorbers

fixes.title: Manually redistribute the load
fixes.description: Open the door and move items around so weight is evenly distributed around the drum.

fixes.title: Adjust load size
fixes.description: For single heavy items like rugs or comforters, add a few towels to counterbalance.

fixes.title: Check the drum suspension
fixes.description: Grab the drum through the door and shake it gently. On a healthy machine, the drum should resist and spring back. Floppy movement means worn shock absorbers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, redistribute laundry, then press Start/Pause to resume. The error clears automatically after successful rebalancing.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UE occurs consistently with normal loads, the shock absorbers or spider arm (drum bearing) may need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — water not filling within expected time
error_codes.detail: The LG washer cannot detect sufficient water entering the drum within the fill time limit. This usually points to a supply issue — the most common causes are closed valves or low water pressure.

causes.description: Supply valve partially or completely closed
Inlet hose screens clogged with sediment

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Make sure both hot and cold valves behind the washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet hose screens
fixes.description: Unscrew the inlet hoses from the back of the washer. Inside each port are small mesh screens — clean them under running water.

fixes.title: Test home water pressure
fixes.description: Run a nearby faucet while the washer tries to fill. If pressure is low throughout the home, the issue is the supply.

fixes.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixes.description: Using a multimeter, test each solenoid coil for continuity (should read 200–500 ohms). Replace if an open circuit is found.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the supply issue, the error clears automatically on the next fill attempt. Press Start/Pause to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If valves and screens are fine but the error persists, the water inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Motor locked — hall sensor or stator fault
error_codes.detail: The motor control system has detected that the drum is not spinning as commanded, or current to the motor is excessive. LG direct-drive motors are generally very reliable, but the hall sensor (which monitors rotor position) is a common failure point.

fixes.title: Reduce load and retry
fixes.description: Remove half the laundry and restart. LE from overloading is common.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes. This resets the motor control firmware.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor is a small sensor near the rotor. It's inexpensive and often the sole cause of LE. Remove the back panel, locate the sensor near the motor, and swap it.
fixes.warning: Unplug washer and lay it on its front to access the motor.

fixes.title: Inspect the rotor and stator
fixes.description: Check for damage to the rotor magnets and stator windings. Replace damaged components.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, then restart with a reduced load. After motor sensor replacement, the code clears on the first successful cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hall sensor replacement doesn't resolve LE, the stator or main control board needs professional evaluation.

error_codes.meaning: Door open or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The washer has detected that the door is not properly closed or the door lock mechanism cannot engage. The washer will not start or will stop mid-cycle for safety. dE1 and dE2 are more specific door lock fault codes.

causes.description: Laundry or fabric caught in the door seal

fixes.title: Check for laundry in the door seal
fixes.description: Pull back the door gasket and check for small items caught between the gasket and drum.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push the door until it clicks definitively.

fixes.title: Inspect the door catch
fixes.description: Look at the door latch hook and the catch bracket. If the plastic is cracked or bent, replace the assembly.

fixes.title: Test the door lock
fixes.description: Use a multimeter to test the door lock switch for continuity when engaged.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure the door is firmly latched and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes and clicks but the error shows, the lock solenoid assembly needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Tub clean reminder — drum needs cleaning
error_codes.detail: This is not an error — it is a maintenance reminder that the drum should be cleaned. LG recommends cleaning the tub every 30 cycles to prevent mold, mildew, and odor buildup. The reminder will clear after running a Tub Clean cycle.

causes.description: Tub clean cycle count reached (every 30 cycles)

fixes.title: Run the Tub Clean cycle
fixes.description: Select 'Tub Clean' on the cycle selector. Add a washing machine cleaner tablet to the drum (not the dispenser). Run the full cycle.
parts: Affresh or LG washing machine cleaner

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a complete Tub Clean cycle. The tCL counter resets automatically after the cycle finishes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Tub Clean is a maintenance reminder, not a fault. No professional service needed.

error_codes.meaning: Water overflow error — drum overfilling
error_codes.detail: The water level sensor has detected that the drum has filled above the maximum safe level. The washer will attempt to drain immediately. This is usually caused by a failed water inlet valve or a malfunctioning pressure sensor.

causes.description: Failed water inlet valve stuck open
Pressure hose to sensor kinked or disconnected

fixes.title: Check the pressure hose
fixes.description: The pressure hose connects the tub to the pressure sensor. Make sure it is not kinked, blocked, or disconnected.

fixes.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the hose from the sensor and gently blow into the hose port — you should hear a click from the sensor.

fixes.title: Test and replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: A stuck-open inlet valve continuously allows water in. Test by turning off the water supply when the error occurs. If water stops, the valve needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off water supply and unplug the washer. Drain remaining water through the pump filter. After repair, restart with water supply restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the inlet valve is replaced and overflow continues, the pressure sensor needs replacement or the control board has a fault.

error_codes.meaning: Duct blockage detected — exhaust duct 80% blocked
error_codes.detail: LG's Flow Sense sensor has detected that your dryer's exhaust duct is approximately 80% blocked. Airflow is significantly reduced, causing longer dry times, overheating risk, and potential fire hazard. This is the single most-searched LG dryer error code with nearly 10,000 searches per month.

causes.description: Lint buildup in exhaust duct over time
Kinked or crushed flexible duct behind dryer
Exterior vent cap clogged or flap stuck closed
Duct run too long or too many 90° bends

fixes.title: Clean the lint filter
fixes.description: Remove the lint filter (inside the door) and clean thoroughly. This should be done after every cycle.

fixes.title: Disconnect and clean the exhaust duct
fixes.description: Pull the dryer from the wall and disconnect the duct from the back. Use a dryer vent brush kit to clean the full length of the duct from both ends.

fixes.title: Check the exterior vent
fixes.description: Go outside and locate the exhaust vent. Remove any lint buildup, bird nests, or debris. Ensure the flap opens freely.

fixes.title: Replace flexible duct with rigid metal
fixes.description: Flexible foil or plastic ducts crush easily and trap lint in their ridges. Replace with smooth rigid metal duct for runs over 3 feet.

fixes.title: Recheck duct routing
fixes.description: Maximum recommended duct run: 25 feet with no more than two 90° elbows. Reduce run length if possible.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the duct, run an Air Only (no heat) cycle for 20 minutes. The D80 code will clear once the Flow Sense sensor detects adequate airflow. If it doesn't clear after a full cycle, blockage remains.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the duct is completely clean and the code persists, the Flow Sense sensor itself may have failed. Professional diagnosis needed.

error_codes.meaning: Duct blockage detected — exhaust duct 90% blocked
error_codes.detail: LG's Flow Sense system has detected a critical 90% duct blockage. At this level, the dryer will reduce heat output or stop the cycle entirely to prevent overheating and fire risk. The D90 code is more urgent than D80 and requires immediate attention. This is the second most-searched LG dryer error with nearly 3,000 monthly searches.

causes.description: Severe lint buildup in exhaust duct
Multiple blockages: lint filter + duct + exterior cap
Duct too long or too many bends causing low flow

fixes.title: Stop using the dryer immediately
fixes.description: A 90% blockage is a fire risk. Do not continue using the dryer until the duct is cleared.
fixes.warning: Fire hazard — stop all drying until resolved.

fixes.title: Deep-clean the entire duct system
fixes.description: Clean the lint filter, the full length of the exhaust duct, the connection point at the dryer, and the exterior vent cap. Use a professional-length dryer vent brush.

fixes.title: Check for duct damage
fixes.description: If the duct is made of flexible plastic or foil, it may have crushed inside a wall. Replace with rigid metal duct. Consider hiring a duct cleaning service for in-wall runs.

fixes.title: Test airflow manually
fixes.description: After cleaning, hold your hand at the exterior vent while the dryer runs on Air Only. You should feel strong airflow.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After completely clearing all blockages, run the dryer on Air Only for 20–30 minutes. The D90 code will clear once adequate airflow is confirmed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct cleaning doesn't clear the code, the Flow Sense sensor may have failed, or you may need a professional duct cleaning service for complex wall-routed duct runs.

error_codes.meaning: Duct blockage detected — exhaust duct 95% blocked
error_codes.detail: Critical duct blockage at 95%. The dryer has nearly no exhaust airflow and is at serious risk of overheating and fire. The dryer may refuse to operate or will only run briefly before shutting down. Treat this as an emergency.

causes.description: Duct physically collapsed or crushed inside wall

fixes.title: Stop using the dryer immediately
fixes.description: 95% blockage is a fire emergency. Do not run the dryer.
fixes.warning: FIRE HAZARD — do not operate until fully resolved.

fixes.title: Call a professional duct cleaning service
fixes.description: At 95% blockage, the duct may have compacted lint that a brush alone won't clear. A professional service uses high-powered vacuum equipment.

fixes.title: Inspect the duct in the wall
fixes.description: If the duct runs inside walls, there may be a physical collapse. You may need to replace the entire run.

error_codes.reset_instructions: The D95 code will clear only after a thorough, professional-level duct cleaning. Run Air Only cycle to confirm airflow is restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: At 95% blockage, a professional duct cleaning service is strongly recommended. DIY cleaning may not be sufficient for compacted lint deep in the duct.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — temperature sensor not reading
error_codes.detail: The dryer's thermistor (temperature sensor) is not reading correctly. The dryer cannot safely regulate heat without accurate temperature data.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: The thermistor is usually on the exhaust duct inside the dryer. At room temp, test resistance: ~10k ohms expected.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the thermistor.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: A simple pull-and-click replacement if the sensor is accessible.
fixes.warning: Unplug dryer first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug for 1 minute then restart. Code clears after a successful cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and error continues, the control board thermistor input is faulty.

error_codes.meaning: Child lock is active — controls are locked
error_codes.detail: The child lock feature has been enabled. All control buttons except Power are disabled. This is not an error — it is an intentional lock mode.

causes.description: Child lock intentionally or accidentally activated

fixes.title: Disable child lock
fixes.description: Press and hold the Child Lock button for 3 seconds until the CL indicator turns off. On some models, press and hold 'Temp' for 3 seconds.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the Child Lock button for 3 seconds to unlock.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Child lock does not require service.

error_codes.meaning: No power to the heating element
error_codes.detail: The dryer is operating but the heating element is not receiving voltage. This typically indicates a wiring issue, a failed heater relay on the control board, or a problem with the home's electrical supply.

causes.description: Tripped circuit breaker on the dryer's 240V circuit
Failed heater relay on main control board
Burned wire or connector to heating element

fixes.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Electric dryers use a 240V circuit with two breaker switches. If one side trips, the dryer runs (motor works on 120V) but won't heat. Reset both breaker switches.

fixes.title: Inspect the heating element wiring
fixes.description: Check the wiring terminals at the heating element for burn marks or loose connections.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and check for continuity. Should read approximately 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Check control board relay
fixes.description: If element and wiring are good, the relay that controls element power has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reset the circuit breaker first. If that doesn't resolve it, unplug dryer for 5 minutes and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Electrical faults (heater relays, control boards) require professional diagnosis if basic checks don't resolve the issue.

error_codes.meaning: Leakage detected — water found in base tray
error_codes.detail: The anti-flood device has detected water in the base tray of the dishwasher. This indicates a leak somewhere in the unit. The LG dishwasher will drain and shut down to prevent water damage.

causes.description: Loose water supply or drain hose connection
Overfill from stuck water inlet valve

fixes.title: Find and drain the base tray
fixes.description: Tilt the dishwasher slightly forward (with power off) to drain water from the base. Use towels.

fixes.title: Check the door seal
fixes.description: Run your hand along the entire door gasket. Replace if cracked, torn, or detached.

fixes.title: Check all hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect supply line, drain hose, and internal hose connections inside the door for looseness.

fixes.title: Inspect the tub
fixes.description: With the unit emptied, look for cracks in the plastic tub, especially at the sump area.

error_codes.reset_instructions: The AE code will clear automatically once the base tray is dry and the source of the leak is fixed. Run a short cycle to confirm no recurrence.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak source is found, the tub or pump housing may have a hairline crack requiring professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The LG dishwasher cannot drain water within the expected time. Water is pooling in the bottom of the tub.

causes.description: Drain filter clogged with food debris
Drain hose kinked or not high-looped
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack. Turn and lift out the filter assembly. Rinse under water, scrubbing debris with a soft brush. Reinstall firmly.

fixes.title: Check drain hose routing
fixes.description: The drain hose must have a high loop under the counter before connecting to the sink drain.

fixes.title: Check garbage disposal connection
fixes.description: If connected to a disposal, run the disposal before the dishwasher cycle.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clean and hose is clear, test the pump for operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, press Start to resume or restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the drain filter is clean and no drain hose obstruction exists, the pump needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — not filling with water
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is not receiving sufficient water. The cycle cannot proceed without water fill.

causes.description: Water supply valve under sink closed

fixes.title: Check the supply valve
fixes.description: Under the sink, confirm the dishwasher supply valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Check for kinks
fixes.description: Trace the supply hose from valve to dishwasher for kinks.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Disconnect the hose at the dishwasher inlet port and clean the mesh filter.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is confirmed good but no fill, replace the inlet valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore water supply and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and still no fill, the inlet valve solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer fan error — fan motor not operating
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan motor in the freezer compartment has failed or is not spinning at the correct speed. Without this fan, cold air cannot circulate from the evaporator coils to the freezer and refrigerator compartments. Food will warm up within hours.

causes.description: Fan blade broken or hitting ice/frost

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Remove the freezer back panel. Look for excessive frost or ice buildup around the fan blade. If present, manually defrost by leaving the refrigerator off for 24–48 hours.

fixes.title: Spin the fan blade
fixes.description: After defrosting, spin the fan blade by hand. It should rotate freely. If it's stiff or hitting something, clear the obstruction.

fixes.title: Test the fan motor
fixes.description: Disconnect the fan and apply 12VDC. If it doesn't spin with direct power, the motor has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: The motor is mounted on a bracket behind the freezer back panel. Unscrew, disconnect, and install new motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, plug in and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize. The error code should clear automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice buildup recurs within weeks after clearing, the defrost heater or defrost control is failing — this needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost heater error — defrost cycle not completing
error_codes.detail: The automatic defrost system has failed to complete a defrost cycle. Over time, frost will accumulate on the evaporator coils, eventually blocking airflow and causing the refrigerator to stop cooling. This is a common LG refrigerator issue often linked to a failed defrost heater or thermal limiter.

causes.description: Thermal fuse (limiter) blown on heater circuit

fixes.title: Manually defrost the coils
fixes.description: Turn off the refrigerator and allow all frost to melt. This provides temporary relief while you diagnose the root cause.

fixes.title: Access the evaporator and defrost heater
fixes.description: Remove the freezer back panel. The defrost heater wraps around or sits near the evaporator coils.

fixes.title: Test the heater for continuity
fixes.description: A working heater shows continuity (a few ohms). Open circuit = burned heater.

fixes.title: Test the thermal limiter
fixes.description: Small bi-metal devices on the heater circuit. Test each for continuity — a blown limiter shows open circuit.

fixes.title: Replace failed components
fixes.description: Install new heater, limiter(s), or thermostat as needed.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator and allow ice to melt before accessing heater.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, the defrost system will run on its regular schedule (every 8–12 hours). The Er dH code should clear after a successful defrost cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If all defrost components test correctly, the main control board defrost control logic has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor error
error_codes.detail: The condenser fan motor (located near the compressor at the back bottom of the refrigerator) has failed. Without this fan, heat cannot be expelled from the condenser coils, causing the compressor to overheat and the refrigerator to stop cooling.

causes.description: Debris or pet hair blocking condenser fan

fixes.title: Clean the condenser coils and fan area
fixes.description: Access the back of the refrigerator and remove the access panel. Vacuum out all dust and pet hair from the fan blades and condenser coils.

fixes.title: Spin the condenser fan blade by hand
fixes.description: With power off, spin the fan blade. It should rotate freely. If stiff, the bearing has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the condenser fan motor
fixes.description: The motor is usually held by a single bracket. Disconnect wiring, unscrew bracket, and install new motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the condenser area.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, plug in and allow several hours for temperatures to stabilize. The code clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan is clean and free but the error persists, the control board fan relay may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low water fill detected — water inlet problem
error_codes.detail: The Whirlpool washer is not filling with enough water within the expected time. This indicates a water supply issue, a clogged inlet screen, or a failing water inlet valve. This is one of the more common Whirlpool front-load washer errors.

causes.description: Inlet hose screen clogged with sediment or debris

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Ensure both hot and cold supply valves behind the washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet hose screens
fixes.description: Disconnect the inlet hoses at the back of the washer. Inside each port are mesh screens — remove and clean them.

fixes.title: Check water pressure
fixes.description: A minimum of 20 PSI is required. Test by running a faucet while the washer fills.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: Disconnect wires from the valve solenoids and test continuity. Should read 200–500 ohms. Replace if open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After resolving the water supply issue, press and hold the Start button for 3 seconds to clear and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good and inlet valve is new but error persists, the flow meter or pressure switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — cycle paused, water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Whirlpool washer attempted to drain but could not complete the drain within the expected time. Standing water will remain in the drum. The drain pump filter is the most common cause and takes only minutes to clean.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the filter behind the access panel on the lower front of the washer. Prepare towels and a shallow pan. Turn cap counterclockwise slowly — water will flow out. Remove and clean the filter.
fixes.warning: Have several towels ready — water will spill.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the drain hose is not kinked and has a proper high loop. The hose should not be inserted more than 8 inches into the standpipe.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Verify the standpipe or sink drain isn't blocked.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear but the washer doesn't drain, test the pump motor for continuity and replace if failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a Drain & Spin cycle. The F9E1 code clears once draining completes successfully.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump replacement doesn't resolve the issue, the main control board drain relay may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — latch cannot engage
error_codes.detail: The door lock mechanism cannot properly engage or disengage. The washer will not start without a confirmed locked door, and will stop immediately if the lock fails mid-cycle.

causes.description: Worn door latch hook or strike plate
Laundry caught between door and gasket
Wiring harness to door lock damaged

fixes.title: Check for items in door seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing or small items lodged in the door gasket preventing full closure.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch
fixes.description: Check the door latch (on the door) and the strike on the machine for wear or damage.

fixes.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixes.description: Remove the door lock assembly and test the solenoid for continuity.

fixes.title: Check wiring
fixes.description: Inspect wiring harness from control board to door lock for damage or loose connectors.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed and restart. If error persists, the door lock assembly needs inspection.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Door lock assembly replacement is a straightforward repair, but if the problem is a wiring harness issue, professional diagnosis is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board failure
error_codes.detail: The primary electronic control board has experienced an internal fault or component failure. This controls all functions of the washer. This error typically requires control board replacement.

causes.description: Voltage spike or power surge damaging control board
Failed component on control board (capacitor, relay)
Moisture or corrosion on control board

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10–15 minutes and restart. Some transient control board faults clear on power cycle.

fixes.title: Check for moisture
fixes.description: Inspect the control board area for signs of water intrusion or corrosion.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the error persists after power cycling, the main PCB needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire positions before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If F1E1 returns immediately, the board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is straightforward but if error returns on new board, there may be a power supply issue causing the failure.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected — too much detergent
error_codes.detail: The washer has detected excess foam. It will pause and wait for suds to reduce before resuming. Over-sudsing can damage the pump and bearings over time.

causes.description: Too much detergent — using more than manufacturer's minimum recommended dose
Non-HE detergent used in HE front-load washer
Detergent residue buildup in dispenser and drum

fixes.title: Wait for suds to clear
fixes.description: The washer will continue automatically once suds subside. This may take up to 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Run Rinse+Spin
fixes.description: If cycle doesn't resume, run a Rinse+Spin only cycle to clear suds.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent going forward
fixes.description: Use only HE-labeled detergent. Use no more than 1 tablespoon per load. Less is more with HE washers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: SUD clears automatically. No manual reset needed. If it recurs, reduce detergent amount significantly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is not a mechanical fault. Persistent SUD with minimal detergent may indicate a water level sensor issue.

error_codes.meaning: Dispenser cannot dispense detergent or additive
error_codes.detail: The dispenser system (drawer or automatic dispenser) is unable to dispense laundry products as programmed. This may affect wash quality but does not stop the cycle on all models.

causes.description: Dispenser clogged with hardened detergent or fabric softener

fixes.title: Clean the dispenser drawer
fixes.description: Remove the dispenser drawer completely and clean thoroughly with warm water, removing all hardened detergent and fabric softener buildup.

fixes.title: Clean the dispenser housing
fixes.description: Use a small brush to clean the compartment where the drawer sits, including the spray nozzles.

fixes.title: Inspect dispenser actuator
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve it, the dispenser actuator motor or solenoid may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, restart the cycle. Run a Rinse+Spin to confirm the dispenser is now functioning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the dispenser is clean but still doesn't function, the dispenser solenoid or motor needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Motor speed error — motor not reaching target RPM
error_codes.detail: The washer motor is not spinning at the required speed. This prevents the spin cycle from reaching the target RPM, leaving clothes wetter than normal.

fixes.title: Reduce load and retry
fixes.description: An overloaded drum prevents the motor from reaching speed. Remove half the laundry.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Test motor and control board
fixes.description: If error persists, professional testing of motor control module is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, reduce load, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor and motor control board diagnosis requires professional testing if basic steps don't resolve the error.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board failure
error_codes.detail: The electronic control board has experienced an internal fault. This is the most searched Whirlpool dryer error code. The control board manages all functions — without it, nothing operates. SERP weakness for this query is 27/100 — a clear opportunity for a comprehensive written guide.

fixes.title: Check for a tripped circuit breaker
fixes.description: Before replacing the board, confirm the dryer is getting full 240V power. A partially tripped breaker can cause board errors.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. Some transient board faults clear this way.

fixes.title: Inspect the control board visually
fixes.description: Access the board (usually behind the front or back panel). Look for burn marks, blown capacitors, or corrosion. If visible damage exists, replacement is needed.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: Remove the old board, photograph all wiring connections, and install new board in reverse order.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire connections before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If F01 reappears immediately, the board has failed and needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F01 returns on a new board, there may be an underlying voltage problem (power surge, bad wiring) causing premature board failure.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The exhaust temperature sensor (thermistor) at the dryer outlet has failed or is reading incorrectly. The dryer cannot properly regulate heat without this sensor.

fixes.title: Locate the thermistor
fixes.description: The outlet thermistor is typically located in the exhaust duct inside the dryer cabinet.

fixes.title: Test resistance
fixes.description: At room temperature: approximately 10,000–12,000 ohms. Test with a multimeter.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect connector and remove sensor mounting screw. Install new sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug for 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and error persists, the control board thermistor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow — vent blockage
error_codes.detail: The dryer has detected insufficient airflow through the exhaust system. This is a safety shutdown — running with blocked airflow causes overheating and is a fire risk.

causes.description: Duct run too long or too many bends

fixes.title: Clean lint filter
fixes.description: Clean after every load.

fixes.title: Clean exhaust duct
fixes.description: Disconnect and clean full duct length with a dryer vent brush.

fixes.title: Clear exterior vent
fixes.description: Check exterior vent for lint, debris, or animal nesting.

fixes.title: Upgrade to rigid duct
fixes.description: Replace flexible foil or plastic duct with rigid metal for any run over 3 feet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the duct, run a 20-minute Air Only cycle. The F30 code clears once adequate airflow is detected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct is completely clear and code persists, the airflow sensor or high-limit thermostat may need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: The drum drive motor has failed. The drum will not rotate, clothes will not tumble, and the dryer cannot complete a cycle.

fixes.title: Test the drive motor
fixes.description: Access the motor (inside the drum cabinet). Test motor windings for continuity. No continuity = burned motor.

fixes.title: Check the thermal overload
fixes.description: Some motors have a thermal overload reset button. Press it and try restart.

fixes.title: Replace the drive motor
fixes.description: Remove the drum to access the motor. Disconnect wiring and drive belt. Install new motor.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After motor replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart. Error clears once motor runs successfully.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive motor replacement requires significant disassembly. If not experienced with appliance repair, professional service is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher drain pump is not draining water within the expected time. Standing water in the tub indicates this error.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the cylindrical filter in the bottom of the tub. Rinse and replace.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure the drain hose has a high loop and is not kinked.

fixes.title: Check disposal knockout
fixes.description: If connected to disposal, confirm knockout plug is removed.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clean, test the pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, press Cancel then Start to run a new cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails continuity test, replace it. Persistent errors after replacement point to a control board relay fault.

error_codes.meaning: Wash heater or heating element failure
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is unable to heat water to the target wash temperature. Dishes will not be sanitized and drying performance will suffer.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access from under the dishwasher. Disconnect wires and test continuity — should read 15–30 ohms.

fixes.title: Test the NTC temperature sensor
fixes.description: Resistance at room temp should be approximately 6k ohms.

fixes.title: Check control board
fixes.description: If both element and sensor are good, the relay on the control board controlling the heater has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run a test cycle. Error clears after a successful heated wash cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If heating element replacement doesn't resolve F6E4, the control board needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan motor in the freezer has stopped functioning. This fan circulates cold air from the evaporator to both the freezer and refrigerator sections. Without it, temperatures will rise throughout the refrigerator.

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Frost buildup is very common. Unplug and let defrost completely before replacing the motor.

fixes.title: Access the evaporator fan
fixes.description: Remove freezer back panel. Fan is mounted on a bracket above or beside the evaporator coils.

fixes.title: Test the motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC to test. If motor doesn't spin, replace it.

fixes.title: Replace the motor
fixes.description: Disconnect wiring, unscrew bracket, swap motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours for temperature stabilization. Code clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice accumulates again within weeks, the defrost heater is likely failing.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The condenser fan (near the compressor at the bottom rear of the refrigerator) has stopped running. The compressor will overheat and shut down, causing the entire refrigerator to stop cooling.

causes.description: Debris or pet hair blocking fan

fixes.title: Clean the condenser area
fixes.description: Remove rear access panel. Vacuum out dust and pet hair from condenser fan and coils.

fixes.title: Spin fan blade by hand
fixes.description: Should rotate freely. Stiff = bearing failure.

fixes.title: Replace condenser fan motor
fixes.description: Disconnect wiring, unscrew from bracket, install new motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow several hours for temperature recovery. Error clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If motor is clean and free but doesn't run with voltage applied, replace it. If new motor still shows error, control board relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The temperature sensor in the freezer compartment has failed. The control board cannot accurately read freezer temperature, which may cause over-cooling or under-cooling.

fixes.title: Locate the freezer thermistor
fixes.description: Usually clipped to the evaporator coil or mounted inside the freezer compartment.

fixes.title: Test resistance
fixes.description: Should read approximately 5k–16k ohms depending on temperature.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Clip off old sensor, install new one at same location.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 5 minutes then restart. Code clears after normal temperature cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor tests good and code persists, the control board sensor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Heater open circuit — heating element has failed
error_codes.detail: The wash water heater has an open circuit, meaning the heating element is burned through. The washer can still run cold water cycles, but hot and warm water settings will not work, and some sanitize cycles will be disabled.

causes.description: Heater wiring harness damaged or disconnected

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect the two wires from the heater. Test with a multimeter for continuity. A good heater reads approximately 20–30 ohms. Open circuit = failed heater.

fixes.title: Check wiring to heater
fixes.description: Inspect both wires leading to the heater for damage, burned insulation, or loose connections.

fixes.title: Replace the heating element
fixes.description: The element is typically accessible from under the tub. Follow service manual for your specific model.
fixes.warning: Unplug and fully drain the washer before accessing the heater.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the heating element, unplug for 5 minutes and restart. The code clears after a successful heated cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating element access requires significant disassembly. Professional service is recommended if you're not experienced.

error_codes.meaning: Water temperature too low
error_codes.detail: The water entering the washer is below the expected temperature for the selected cycle. This can indicate a water heater issue in the home, a cross-connected supply hose, or a failing water temperature sensor.

causes.description: Home water heater set too low or needs time to deliver hot water
Hot and cold supply hoses connected in reverse
Water inlet valve not opening hot side

fixes.title: Check hot water supply
fixes.description: Run the hot water faucet at a nearby sink and feel the temperature. If cold, the home water heater has an issue.

fixes.title: Verify hose connections
fixes.description: Make sure the hot hose (usually red coded) connects to the hot supply valve and hot port on the washer.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: The thermistor reads incoming water temperature. Test resistance and compare to spec.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the supply issue or replacing the sensor, restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hot water is confirmed good at the tap but E61 persists, the NTC sensor or inlet valve hot side needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heater relay failure on main control board
error_codes.detail: The relay on the main control board that switches power to the heating element has failed. The element may be physically fine, but the board cannot send power to it.

causes.description: Heater relay stuck open on control board

fixes.title: Confirm heater element is good
fixes.description: Test element first (see E64) to confirm the element itself is not the issue before replacing the board.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If element tests OK, the board relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If E65 returns with a known-good heater element, the board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement for GE washers is complex. Professional diagnosis is recommended to confirm the board and not the element is the cause.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor (water temperature sensor) has an open circuit and is not providing temperature readings to the control board. The washer's heating function is disabled.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect and test resistance with multimeter. Room temperature reading should be approximately 6k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat connector firmly at the thermistor mounting.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Install new thermistor and ensure it's properly clipped to the heater or drum housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and tests correctly but error persists, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Spin speed error — motor not reaching target RPM
error_codes.detail: The washer motor is not spinning the drum at the commanded speed. Clothes will exit the cycle still wet.

causes.description: Drum overloaded preventing motor from reaching speed

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items and retry with a smaller load.

fixes.title: Check drive belt
fixes.description: On belt-driven models, inspect the belt for wear, stretching, or breakage.

fixes.title: Inspect motor brushes
fixes.description: On brushed DC motors, worn brushes cause speed loss. Replace if worn below 1/4 inch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a Spin Only cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If belt and brushes are fine and error persists, motor control board testing is required.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The GE dishwasher cannot drain water within the expected time. Standing water remains in the tub.

causes.description: Drain filter clogged with food particles
Drain hose kinked or improperly high-looped

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the filter at the bottom of the tub (twist counterclockwise) and rinse clean.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the hose runs up to the counter underside before dropping to the drain.

fixes.title: Check the disposal
fixes.description: Run the disposal and confirm the connection point is clear.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If all above clear, test pump motor for operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run a rinse cycle to confirm drain.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump is running but water doesn't drain, the pump impeller may be broken requiring pump replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Flood switch activated — water in base tray
error_codes.detail: The flood protection switch has detected water in the base of the dishwasher. The unit will not run until the water is cleared and the leak source is fixed.

fixes.title: Tilt unit to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt the dishwasher forward (with power off) to let base water drain.
