fixes.warning: Disconnect power at breaker before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check entire perimeter of door gasket for damage.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect supply line and drain hose connections inside the unit.

fixes.title: Check detergent amount
fixes.description: Use only dishwasher-specific detergent, 1 tablespoon max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base tray, fix leak source, and restart. Code clears once sensor is dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak is visible, the tub itself may be cracked, requiring professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Wash motor failure — circulation pump not running
error_codes.detail: The main circulation pump motor has failed or is drawing incorrect current. The dishwasher cannot perform a wash cycle.

causes.description: Foreign object jammed in pump impeller

fixes.title: Check for debris in pump
fixes.description: Remove filter and reach into sump to clear any debris from impeller.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — broken glass may be present.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace circulation pump
fixes.description: If motor is burned, full pump assembly replacement is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing jams, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement requires significant disassembly. Professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted by power outage
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher detected a power interruption during a cycle. This is not a fault — it is an informational code. The cycle needs to be restarted.

causes.description: Power outage or interruption during cycle

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Press Start to resume or select a new cycle.

fixes.title: Check circuit breaker
fixes.description: If power interruptions are frequent, check the dishwasher's dedicated circuit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start to begin a new cycle. PF clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: PF does not require service unless power interruptions are frequent — in that case, check household wiring.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor issue — freezer not defrosting correctly
error_codes.detail: The defrost sensor (which monitors the evaporator temperature during defrost cycles) has failed or is reading incorrectly. This can lead to frost buildup on the evaporator coils and eventual cooling failure.

fixes.title: Defrost the refrigerator manually
fixes.description: Unplug and allow complete defrost (24–48 hours). Check if code clears after full defrost.

fixes.title: Locate and test the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Behind the freezer back panel, on or near the evaporator. Test resistance.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Unclip from evaporator and install replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, allow 24 hours for normal operation to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If defrost sensor is new and error continues, the defrost heater or defrost timer/board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer compartment temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor measuring freezer temperature has failed. Without accurate freezer temperature data, the control board cannot regulate compressor operation correctly.

fixes.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixes.description: In a 32°F/0°C environment: approx 16k ohms.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: The connector is often the issue — unplug and firmly reseat.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Install new sensor in same clip location.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 5 minutes and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both sensors fail simultaneously, the control board or wiring harness has a common fault.

error_codes.meaning: Demo/showroom mode active — cooling is disabled
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has been placed in showroom display mode. All lights and controls work, but the compressor and cooling system are off. Food will spoil in this mode.

fixes.title: Exit demo mode
fixes.description: On most GE models: press and hold 'Lock' and 'Ice Maker' simultaneously for 3 seconds. Or hold 'Freezer' and 'Refrigerator' temp buttons for 6 seconds.

fixes.title: Check your model's manual
fixes.description: If the standard combination doesn't work, consult the manual for your specific model number.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Follow the demo mode exit procedure for your model. The display will return to actual temperatures.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If demo mode cannot be exited with standard button combinations, the control board may have a software issue.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — water not draining within expected time
error_codes.detail: The Bosch washing machine cannot drain water within the allowed time. This halts the cycle and leaves water in the drum.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: The filter is behind the lower-front service flap. Turn cap slowly to drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — significant water will drain.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Inspect hose for kinks from back of machine to standpipe.

fixes.title: Check standpipe
fixes.description: Pour water directly into standpipe. If it backs up, clear the household drain.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clear and hose is good, pump motor may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, press the pause/cancel button to reset, then restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump is replaced and E18 persists, control board drain relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — cannot lock door for cycle
error_codes.detail: The washer door lock mechanism cannot engage. The washer will not start without a confirmed locked door.

fixes.title: Check door seal for caught items
fixes.description: Pull back the rubber gasket seal and inspect for trapped clothing.

fixes.title: Inspect door latch
fixes.description: Look for cracked or worn plastic on door latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test door lock solenoid
fixes.description: Disconnect and test solenoid coil for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed and locked. Restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes and clicks but E02 persists, door lock solenoid replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault — fill time exceeded
error_codes.detail: The Bosch washer did not reach the required water level within the expected fill time. Water supply issues are almost always the cause.

causes.description: Inlet hose kinked or screen blocked

fixes.title: Open supply valves fully
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold valves are completely open.

fixes.title: Clean hose screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses at machine connection and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity — 200–500 ohms expected.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply issue and press Start to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists after inlet valve replacement, the pressure switch is likely faulty.

error_codes.meaning: Anti-flood device activated — water detected in base
error_codes.detail: The Bosch AquaStop system has detected water in the base tray of the washing machine. This is a safety shutdown.

fixes.title: Tilt machine to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt machine forward to drain water from base tray. Power off first.
fixes.warning: Turn off water and power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect supply hose fittings
fixes.description: Check hose connections at wall and at machine for any weeping.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for tears or detachment.

fixes.title: Inspect internal hoses
fixes.description: Access back panel and check internal hose connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, allow to dry. Code clears when float sensor resets.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak is visible, internal tub or pump housing crack needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Leak protection activated — water detected in base tray
error_codes.detail: The AquaStop anti-flood system has detected water in the base tray of the Bosch dishwasher. This is one of the most searched Bosch dishwasher error codes. The dishwasher will pump water out continuously and lock itself until the issue is resolved.

causes.description: Overfilled with water — door seal worn or torn
Sump or pump housing crack allowing water into base
Condensation or steam accumulation triggering float sensor
Internal hose connection loose or cracked
Using too much detergent causing foam overflow

fixes.title: Tilt the dishwasher to drain the base
fixes.description: With power off at the breaker, carefully tilt the dishwasher forward about 45 degrees. Water will drain from the base pan. Place towels down. Let it sit tilted for 1–2 minutes.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before tilting. The unit is heavy — get assistance.

fixes.title: Allow the base to dry
fixes.description: After draining, leave the dishwasher upright with the door open for several hours to allow any remaining moisture to evaporate from the base.

fixes.title: Check the door seal
fixes.description: Run your fingers along the full perimeter of the rubber door gasket. Look for cracks, tears, or sections that have pulled away from the channel. Replace if damaged.

fixes.title: Check for oversudsing
fixes.description: If you used too much detergent, suds overflow into the base. Use only recommended dishwasher detergent — 1 tablespoon maximum.

fixes.title: Inspect internal hose connections
fixes.description: If the above don't apply, access the inside of the door panel and check the water inlet, circulation, and drain hoses for cracks or loose connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Tilt the unit to drain the base, allow it to dry completely, then restart. If the leak has been resolved, the float sensor will reset and the E15 code will clear.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If E15 recurs after drying and the door seal is intact, there is an internal water leak (cracked sump, hose, or pump) requiring professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Drain blocked — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The Bosch dishwasher cannot drain water. This is a very common error often caused by a blocked drain filter or a kinked drain hose. It's usually a quick DIY fix.

causes.description: Clogged drain filter and mesh screen
Garbage disposal connection blocked or knockout not removed
Drain pump impeller blocked by glass or debris

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter system
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack. Unscrew the coarse filter (twist counterclockwise), then lift out the flat fine mesh filter. Rinse both thoroughly under running water using a brush.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Pull the dishwasher out slightly and trace the drain hose. It should loop up to the underside of the counter and not be kinked.

fixes.title: Check garbage disposal
fixes.description: Run the disposal before starting the dishwasher. If newly installed, check that the knockout plug was removed.

fixes.title: Check pump impeller
fixes.description: If filter is clean and hose is clear, reach into the sump and feel around the pump opening for broken glass or hard debris blocking the impeller.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — broken glass may be present.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, press the On/Off button for 3 seconds to reset, then run a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but the pump still doesn't drain (you can hear it trying), the pump motor has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump or filter blocked — drain impeller obstructed
error_codes.detail: The drain pump filter or pump impeller is physically blocked by debris. This is a more specific version of E24 pointing to the pump or filter area rather than the hose.

causes.description: Hard debris (bone, toothpick, glass) blocking pump impeller
Accumulated food debris in filter basket

fixes.title: Clean filter and remove impeller debris
fixes.description: Follow the same steps as E24. Pay particular attention to clearing the pump impeller area.
fixes.warning: Use gloves when reaching into the pump area.

fixes.title: Check pump impeller rotation
fixes.description: After clearing, reach in and try to spin the pump impeller. It should rotate freely.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump if impeller is damaged
fixes.description: If impeller is broken or pump motor is seized, replace the pump assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing debris, press On/Off for 3 seconds to reset then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump is replaced and E25 persists, there is a control board drain relay fault.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element or NTC sensor failure — water not heating
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher cannot heat water to the target temperature. The heating element or the temperature sensor monitoring it has failed. Dishes will not be properly sanitized.

causes.description: Heater relay on control board failed

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access from under the dishwasher. Disconnect element terminals. Should read 15–25 ohms. Open circuit = element failure.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature: approximately 6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install new element or sensor as needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run a test cycle on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and sensor both test OK, the control board heater relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Heating circuit failure — heater not functioning
error_codes.detail: The heating circuit has an electrical fault. Similar to E01 but specifically indicates a relay or circuit board issue rather than the element itself.

causes.description: Heater relay failure on main PCB

fixes.title: Test heating element first
fixes.description: Rule out element failure before replacing the board.

fixes.title: Check wiring to heater
fixes.description: Inspect element wiring for burn marks or melted insulation.

fixes.title: Replace control board or relay
fixes.description: If element is good, the PCB heater control circuit has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart on a short test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is complex. Professional diagnosis recommended to confirm it's the board and not a wiring issue.

error_codes.meaning: Water shortage — insufficient water entering dishwasher
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is not receiving adequate water to fill for the wash cycle.

causes.description: Water supply valve under sink closed or partially closed
Inlet mesh filter blocked with scale

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixes.description: Check for kinks from the wall to the dishwasher.

fixes.title: Clean inlet mesh filter
fixes.description: Unscrew the hose from the dishwasher and clean the small mesh filter inside the inlet port.

fixes.title: Test and replace inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid for continuity. Should read 200–500 ohms.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore water supply and press Start to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water supply is confirmed good and error persists, inlet valve replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure — oven not heating
error_codes.detail: The bake or broil heating element has failed. The oven will not heat up, or heating will be uneven.

causes.description: Heating element burned through (visible break or blistering)
Element wiring harness connection burned or loose

fixes.title: Inspect heating element visually
fixes.description: Open the oven and look at the bake element (bottom) and broil element (top). A burned-through element will have a visible break, hole, or blistered area.

fixes.title: Test element continuity
fixes.description: After allowing oven to cool, disconnect element terminals and test with multimeter. Good element reads 15–30 ohms. Open circuit = failed.

fixes.title: Replace the element
fixes.description: Unscrew the mounting screws, pull the element forward, disconnect the wires, and connect the new element.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool completely and disconnect power at the breaker.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, turn power back on and test with a short bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the element looks intact and tests with correct resistance but the oven still won't heat, the control board relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor failure — NTC sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor (NTC probe) has failed. Without accurate temperature readings, the oven cannot regulate heat properly — it may overheat or not heat at all.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe burned out or open circuit
Probe connector loose at control board
Wiring from probe to board damaged

fixes.title: Test the temperature probe
fixes.description: The probe is usually a small pointed sensor inside the oven cavity. Test resistance at room temperature — should read approximately 1,000–1,100 ohms.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: The probe connects to the control board via a 2-wire connector. Ensure it is firmly seated.

fixes.title: Replace the probe
fixes.description: Unscrew from the oven back wall and replace with a new probe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the probe, run the oven on a short bake cycle. The error should not return.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the probe is new and tests correctly but E111 persists, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Safety cutout tripped — oven overheated
error_codes.detail: The oven exceeded its maximum safe operating temperature and the thermal safety cutout has tripped. This is a critical safety code. Do not continue using the oven until the underlying cause is found.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe failure causing control board to keep heating
Cooling fan failure causing heat buildup

fixes.title: Do not use the oven
fixes.description: Stop all oven use until this is diagnosed.
fixes.warning: SAFETY CRITICAL — do not operate the oven until diagnosed.

fixes.title: Allow oven to cool completely
fixes.description: Wait at least 2 hours before any inspection.

fixes.title: Test the temperature probe
fixes.description: A failed probe can cause the control board to keep heating without limit. Test resistance (1,000–1,100 ohms at room temp).

fixes.title: Check the cooling fan
fixes.description: Ovens have a cooling fan. Listen for it running when the oven is on. No sound = failed fan.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Do not reset and continue using. This code requires diagnosing the underlying overheating cause before operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: E132 is a safety event requiring professional service before further oven use. The root cause must be identified to prevent recurrence.

error_codes.meaning: Low water supply — insufficient fill detected
error_codes.detail: KitchenAid washers share the Whirlpool platform. F8E1 indicates the washer is not detecting adequate water fill within the timeout period. Supply valve, inlet screens, and water pressure are the first things to check.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both valves behind washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity (200–500 ohms).

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply issue, press and hold Start for 3 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is good and valve is new, flow meter needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The KitchenAid washer cannot drain within the expected time. The pump filter is almost always the culprit.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Behind lower access panel. Drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure hose is not kinked and has proper high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear, test pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run Drain & Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pump replacement needed if motor tests failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The door lock cannot engage or disengage properly. The washer will not operate with an unlocked door.

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Look for laundry caught in gasket.

fixes.title: Inspect latch
fixes.description: Check for cracked plastic on latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test lock solenoid
fixes.description: Test for continuity with multimeter.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is firmly latched and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but error persists, lock assembly needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected
error_codes.detail: Too much foam detected in the wash drum. Cycle paused while suds dissipate.

fixes.title: Wait for suds to clear
fixes.description: Washer will resume automatically within 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: Use HE detergent only; max 1 tablespoon per load.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clears automatically. No reset needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a mechanical fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The drain pump motor has failed or is blocked. Water remains standing in the tub.

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean filter assembly under the bottom rack.

fixes.title: Check drain hose routing
fixes.description: Confirm high-loop installation.

fixes.title: Clear pump impeller
fixes.description: Remove filter and check pump opening for debris.
fixes.warning: Use gloves.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump
fixes.description: If motor is failed, replace pump assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, cancel and restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor tested failed, replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Stuck relay on control board
error_codes.detail: A relay on the main control board is stuck in a closed or open position. This usually requires control board replacement.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If error persists, board relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If error returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement requires professional service if complex wiring is involved.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — water temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor measuring wash water temperature has failed. Heating function disabled.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Resistance at room temp: ~6k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Simple sensor replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan in the freezer has stopped. Cold air cannot circulate to either compartment.

fixes.title: Defrost the freezer
fixes.description: Unplug and fully defrost before replacing motor.

fixes.title: Test fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC — if no spin, motor failed.

fixes.title: Replace motor
fixes.description: Access behind freezer back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours temperature stabilization.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice recurs quickly, defrost heater is failing.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The condenser fan at the back/bottom of the refrigerator has failed. Compressor will overheat.

fixes.title: Clean condenser area
fixes.description: Vacuum out dust from fan and coils.

fixes.title: Test fan
fixes.description: Spin by hand — should be free. Test with 12VDC.

fixes.title: Replace motor
fixes.description: Remove access panel, swap motor assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow several hours for temperature recovery.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If clean and free but no spin, motor failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Frigidaire washer cannot drain water within the programmed time. The pump filter is the most likely cause.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access filter at front lower panel. Drain water, remove and rinse filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure hose has high loop and is not kinked.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: Test motor continuity if filter is clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails continuity test, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Fill time exceeded — water not filling
error_codes.detail: The washer did not reach the required water level within the fill time limit.

fixes.title: Open supply valves
fixes.description: Check both hot and cold valves are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh filters.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Door open — lid or door not properly closed
error_codes.detail: The washer cannot detect a closed and locked door. Cycle will not start or will pause.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing caught in the door gasket.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push until you hear a definite click.

fixes.title: Inspect and replace latch
fixes.description: If latch is worn, replace assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door is closed but E41 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock control board failure
error_codes.detail: The door lock control board has failed. This requires replacing either the door lock assembly or the main control board.

causes.description: Main control board door lock relay

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Test door lock assembly
fixes.description: If lock tests failed, replace assembly.

fixes.title: Replace control board if needed
fixes.description: If door lock tests OK, main board door lock relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If error returns, door lock or board replacement needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both door lock and board are replaced and error continues, wiring harness has a fault.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The electronic control board has experienced an internal fault. This is the main PCB that controls all dryer operations.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If error returns, board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If E11 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is straightforward but requires photographing all wire positions.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure
error_codes.detail: The dryer heating element has failed. The drum turns but produces no heat.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect wires, test continuity. Should read 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Replace element
fixes.description: Access element from back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

fixes.title: Check board relay
fixes.description: If element OK, board heater relay failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element replaced and no heat, control board relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain problem — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Frigidaire dishwasher cannot drain water. Standing water remains in the tub.

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the filter in the dishwasher bottom.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm high loop and no kinks.

fixes.title: Check disposal
fixes.description: Run disposal before cycle.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear, test pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, cancel and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Leak detected in base
error_codes.detail: Water has entered the base pan of the dishwasher, triggering the flood protection switch.

fixes.title: Drain base by tilting
fixes.description: Tilt dishwasher forward (power off) to drain base water.
fixes.warning: Power off before tilting.

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Inspect gasket for tears.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect all internal hose fittings.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: Use only dishwasher detergent, 1 tbsp max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, dry, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no visible leak found, internal crack needs professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Water supply problem — insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is not detecting adequate water fill.

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is open.

fixes.title: Check hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to unit.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply confirmed good.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply issue and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted
error_codes.detail: Power was interrupted during the wash cycle.

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Press Start to run a new cycle.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start. PF clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature alarm — refrigerator or freezer above safe temperature
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has detected that compartment temperature is above the safe threshold. Food safety may be compromised if this has persisted for more than 2 hours.

causes.description: Door left open or not sealing properly

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Press paper between door and frame. If it pulls out easily, the seal is worn.

fixes.title: Clean condenser coils
fixes.description: Vacuum the condenser coils behind or under the unit.

fixes.title: Check fans
fixes.description: Listen for both the condenser fan (back/bottom) and evaporator fan (inside freezer) running.

fixes.title: Allow time to recover
fixes.description: If door was accidentally left open, close it and wait 4 hours.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly. H1 clears when temperature returns to normal range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans are running, coils are clean, and door seals are good but temperature stays high, the compressor or refrigerant needs professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan has stopped functioning. Without it, cold air cannot circulate and temperatures will rise.

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Unplug and fully defrost the freezer before diagnosing the motor.

fixes.title: Test fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC — if no spin, motor has failed.

fixes.title: Replace fan motor
fixes.description: Access behind freezer back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Allow 24 hours after repair for temperature stabilization.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice accumulates again within weeks, defrost heater is failing.

error_codes.meaning: Runaway temperature — oven overheating
error_codes.detail: The oven has detected a runaway temperature condition, meaning it is heating beyond the safe range. This is a critical safety error. Stop using the oven immediately.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe failure causing uncontrolled heating

fixes.title: Stop oven use immediately
fixes.description: Do not use the oven until the fault is diagnosed.
fixes.warning: FIRE HAZARD — stop oven use immediately.

fixes.title: Test the oven temperature probe
fixes.description: The probe should read ~1,100 ohms at room temperature. Out of spec = replace.

fixes.title: Replace control board if probe is OK
fixes.description: If probe tests fine, the board's temperature control relay has stuck.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Do not reset and continue using. Diagnose the underlying cause first.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F10 is a safety event. Professional diagnosis required before returning the oven to service.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature probe failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor (probe) has failed with an open circuit. The oven cannot safely regulate temperature.

fixes.title: Test probe resistance
fixes.description: Should read ~1,100 ohms at room temperature.

fixes.title: Replace probe
fixes.description: Unscrew from oven back wall and install new probe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing probe, run oven on short bake cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If probe is new and tests correctly, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted key — button stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: A key on the oven control panel is registering as continuously pressed. This prevents normal operation.

causes.description: Stuck button from grease or moisture

fixes.title: Clean control panel
fixes.description: Clean around all buttons with dry cloth. Check for sticky residue.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace keypad
fixes.description: If a button remains stuck, replace the membrane keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F11 returns on the same key, that key is shorted.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If keypad replacement doesn't resolve F11, the control board has a shorted keypad input.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining
error_codes.detail: Maytag washers share the Whirlpool platform. F9E1 means the machine cannot drain within the expected time. Pump filter clog is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Access via lower front panel. Drain water and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm no kinks and proper high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear, test pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run Drain & Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pump replacement needed if motor tests failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low water fill detected
error_codes.detail: The washer is not detecting adequate water during fill cycle.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply issue and hold Start for 3 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Main electronic control board failure
error_codes.detail: The primary control board has detected an internal fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If error returns, board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If error returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement requires photographing all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds — too much detergent
error_codes.detail: Washer has detected oversudsing. Cycle paused.

fixes.title: Wait
fixes.description: Washer resumes automatically within 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: HE detergent only, 1 tbsp max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a mechanical fault.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic control board failure
error_codes.detail: The main dryer control board has experienced an internal fault and needs replacement.

fixes.title: Check circuit breaker
fixes.description: Confirm dryer has full 240V power.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If F01 returns, board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If F01 returns immediately, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F01 persists on new board, there may be a wiring or power supply issue.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor (temperature sensor) failure
error_codes.detail: The exhaust temperature sensor has failed. Dryer cannot regulate heat safely.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct inside cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure — drum not turning
error_codes.detail: The drive motor has failed. The drum will not rotate.

fixes.title: Test drive motor
fixes.description: Test motor windings for continuity.

fixes.title: Check thermal overload
fixes.description: Press reset button on motor if present.

fixes.title: Replace drive motor
fixes.description: Remove drum to access motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After motor replacement, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Slow drain — pump is draining but too slowly
error_codes.detail: Kenmore washers are made by Whirlpool or LG depending on the model series. F21 on Whirlpool-platform models means a slow or failed drain. Check the pump filter first.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access panel at lower front. Drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper high loop installation.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear and hose free, pump may be weak or failing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run Drain & Spin cycle after clearing blockage.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump filter cleaning doesn't resolve F21, the pump may need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water supply error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: The washer is not detecting water entering the drum. Usually a supply issue.

fixes.title: Open supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity (200–500 ohms).

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply and restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: On LG-manufactured Kenmore models (typically 400+ series), OE indicates a drain failure. Pump filter and drain hose are the first checks.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Front lower panel filter. Drain water, clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; correct height (28–47 inches).

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear, listen for pump operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run Spin Only cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump filter and hose are clear but no draining, pump motor has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor locked (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: Motor stall or hall sensor fault on LG-manufactured Kenmore washers. The drum cannot rotate at the commanded speed.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove half the items and retry.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor is a small sensor near the rotor — inexpensive and often the sole cause.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes, reduce load, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hall sensor replacement doesn't resolve LE, stator or main board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: The load is unevenly distributed. Spin cycle cannot complete safely.

fixes.title: Redistribute laundry
fixes.description: Open door, move items evenly around drum.

fixes.title: Adjust load size
fixes.description: Remove or add items to balance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Redistribute and press Start/Pause to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UE occurs every load, suspension springs are worn.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: The washer cannot fill with water within the expected time.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Fully open both hot and cold valves.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply confirmed good.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Electrolux washer has failed to drain within the expected time. Pump filter is the first place to check.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Behind lower access panel. Drain water, remove and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm no kinks and proper high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: Test motor if filter is clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run Spin Only cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor tested failed, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: The washer is not detecting water entering during fill. Supply valve, inlet screens, and inlet valve are the common causes.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply confirmed and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Door not closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The washer's door has not been detected as fully closed and locked.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Inspect gasket for caught clothing.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push until definite click.

fixes.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixes.description: If latch or solenoid is failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but E40 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor failure
error_codes.detail: The drive motor has failed or is not operating correctly. The drum will not rotate.

fixes.title: Check for belt (belt-drive models)
fixes.description: Inspect belt for breakage.

fixes.title: Test motor
fixes.description: Access motor, test winding continuity.

fixes.title: Replace motor or control board
fixes.description: Replace whichever component failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Wiring harness fault — communication error
error_codes.detail: The main wiring harness has a fault. This is typically a wiring or board communication issue.

fixes.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixes.description: Check all connector plugs for loose fit, especially at the control board and motor.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace wiring harness
fixes.description: If connectors are secure but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E90 returns, wiring harness needs inspection.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If harness is intact, control board communication port has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Insufficient heating — dryer not reaching temperature
error_codes.detail: The dryer is running but not heating adequately. Clothes emerge still damp.

fixes.title: Check for vent blockage
fixes.description: A blocked vent causes high-limit to trip, cutting heat.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Test for continuity — should read 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Test for continuity. Tripped thermostat reads open.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install new element or thermostat as needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and thermostat are good, board heater relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The dryer heating element has burned through. No heat will be produced.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test for continuity. Open circuit = failed.

fixes.title: Replace element
fixes.description: Access from back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug dryer before accessing element.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element replaced and no heat, board heater relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The dryer's temperature sensor (thermistor) has failed.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct inside cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Pump fault — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Miele washer pump has encountered a fault. Water is not draining correctly.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Behind lower service flap. Turn slowly and drain water before removing filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; correct standpipe height.

fixes.title: Remove impeller obstruction
fixes.description: Check pump housing for foreign objects.

fixes.title: Replace pump
fixes.description: Miele pumps are high-quality but can fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run a Drain/Short Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele service intervals are long, but pump filter should be cleaned every 6 months.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — slow or failed drain
error_codes.detail: The drain cycle is taking longer than allowed. Water is not emptying from the drum.

fixes.title: Clean filter
fixes.description: Same as F11 procedure.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Trace hose for kinks.

fixes.title: Replace pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele authorized service recommended for pump replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water protection system activated — flood protection triggered
error_codes.detail: The Miele waterproof system (Waterproof System/WPS) has detected water in the base of the machine. This is a safety lockout.

fixes.title: Tilt machine to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt forward (power off) to drain base tray.
fixes.warning: Power off water and electricity first.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for tears or pulled sections.

fixes.title: Check supply hose fittings
fixes.description: Inspect both ends of the supply hose for weeping.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak source, dry thoroughly. Sensor resets when base is dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no leak source found, internal hose or sump connection needs Miele authorized inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Motor fault — drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: The Miele washing machine motor has experienced a fault. This is a serious error typically requiring professional service. Miele motors are brushless and exceptionally durable, but can fail after many years.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes. A transient motor fault may clear.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items — F53 can sometimes be triggered by extreme overloading.

fixes.title: Contact Miele service
fixes.description: F53 motor faults on Miele machines almost always require authorized technician diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F53 persists, contact Miele authorized service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele motor replacement is complex and typically requires factory-trained technicians. DIY motor replacement risks voiding the warranty.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The water temperature sensor (NTC thermistor) has failed or is reading out of spec.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: At room temp: should read approximately 6k–10k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Miele genuine parts recommended.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, restart. Code clears after successful heated cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele thermistors are reliable. If new part fails quickly, there may be a wiring issue.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet timeout — dishwasher not filling
error_codes.detail: The Miele dishwasher did not reach the required water level within the fill time limit.

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Check inlet hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to unit.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screen
fixes.description: Unscrew supply hose and clean the screen at the inlet port.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: Miele inlet valves are reliable but do fail after many years.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply issue and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good, Miele authorized service recommended for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: No discharge — drain problem
error_codes.detail: The Miele dishwasher cannot drain water within the expected time.

fixes.title: Clean the filters
fixes.description: Miele dishwashers have a fine filter and coarse filter. Remove and clean both.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure proper high-loop installation and no kinks.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear, pump may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, restart on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele drain pump replacement is best handled by authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Heating issue — water not heating to target temperature
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is not heating the wash water to the required temperature.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity — should read 15–30 ohms.

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: At room temp: ~6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install appropriate replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating component replacement on Miele requires care to maintain waterproof sealing.

error_codes.meaning: Flow meter fault — water quantity sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The flow meter measuring water entering the dishwasher has failed. Water quantity cannot be accurately controlled.

fixes.title: Clean flow meter
fixes.description: The flow meter can be removed and cleaned to clear scale deposits.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat the flow meter connector.

fixes.title: Replace flow meter
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart on short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele flow meters are repairable. Authorized Miele service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet valve fault — water supply issue
error_codes.detail: Speed Queen washers are extremely reliable commercial-grade machines for home use. E3 indicates the water inlet valve is not functioning correctly or water pressure is insufficient.

causes.description: Supply valve closed or partially closed

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold valves are fully open.

fixes.title: Test water pressure
fixes.description: Minimum 20 PSI required.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity (200–500 ohms).

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve if failed
fixes.description: Speed Queen parts are available from authorized dealers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen machines are built for longevity. If valve fails, genuine SQ parts are recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump fault — drainage problem
error_codes.detail: The drain pump has failed or is obstructed. Water is not draining correctly.

fixes.title: Access pump filter
fixes.description: Speed Queen front-loaders have a pump filter. Drain water and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper installation.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump
fixes.description: Speed Queen pumps are commercial-grade but can fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Authorized Speed Queen service is available through their dealer network.

error_codes.meaning: Motor feedback error — motor control fault
error_codes.detail: The motor control system has detected incorrect feedback from the drive motor. This is a serious error typically requiring technical diagnosis.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check motor belt (if applicable)
fixes.description: Some SQ models use a belt drive.

fixes.title: Contact Speed Queen service
fixes.description: Speed Queen machines are built to last 25 years — motor faults typically warrant authorized service.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E2 returns, Speed Queen authorized service is recommended.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen's commercial heritage means complex motor faults are best handled by their dealer service network.

error_codes.meaning: Lid switch fault (top-load models)
error_codes.detail: The lid switch is not detecting the lid as closed. The washer will not spin with an open or faulty lid switch.

causes.description: Lid switch actuator bent or broken

fixes.title: Inspect lid switch actuator
fixes.description: The plastic tab that depresses the switch when the lid closes. Check for cracks.

fixes.title: Test lid switch
fixes.description: Depress switch manually and listen for click. Test with multimeter for continuity.

fixes.title: Replace lid switch
fixes.description: Unscrew switch from lid frame and install replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, close lid firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new lid switch doesn't resolve E5, the wiring harness to the switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — control board fault
error_codes.detail: The main controller cannot communicate with a sub-module or the user interface. This typically indicates a control board issue.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check wiring harness connectors
fixes.description: Reseat all connectors on the control board.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If power cycle and connections don't resolve it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E1 returns, contact Speed Queen authorized service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen machines are repairable for life — authorized dealers stock parts for decades.

error_codes.meaning: Heating error — dryer not heating correctly
error_codes.detail: The Speed Queen dryer's heating system is not functioning correctly. The drum may turn but produces no or insufficient heat.

causes.description: Tripped high-limit thermostat from blocked vent

fixes.title: Check and clean vent
fixes.description: A blocked vent causes high-limit thermostat to trip permanently.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity — 10–20 ohms expected.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Test for continuity — failed thermostat reads open.

fixes.title: Replace failed part
fixes.description: Install genuine Speed Queen replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing heating components.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If vent, element, and thermostat all check out, the control board heater relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The dryer's temperature sensor (thermistor) has failed.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — cycle cannot complete
error_codes.detail: Amana washers are built on the Whirlpool platform. F9E1 means the washer failed to drain within the expected time. Pump filter cleaning resolves this in most cases.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access lower front panel. Drain water and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm high loop and no kinks.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: Test motor if filter is clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run Drain & Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pump replacement needed if motor tests failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low water supply
error_codes.detail: The washer is not detecting sufficient water filling.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Clean mesh screens at washer inlet ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply and press Start.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds
error_codes.detail: Too much foam detected. Cycle paused while suds dissipate.

fixes.title: Wait
fixes.description: Washer resumes automatically.

fixes.title: Use less detergent
fixes.description: HE detergent only, 1 tablespoon max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a mechanical fault.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The main electronic control board has failed.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace board
fixes.description: If F01 returns.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If F01 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board replacement requires photographing all wiring first.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: Exhaust temperature sensor has failed.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature alarm — temperatures above safe range
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has detected temperatures above the safe threshold for food storage. Check the door seals and fans.

causes.description: Door left open or seal worn

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Check all door gaskets for proper sealing.

fixes.title: Clean condenser coils
fixes.description: Vacuum condenser coils at back/bottom of unit.

fixes.title: Check fans
fixes.description: Confirm both evaporator and condenser fans are running.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close doors and wait 4 hours. H1 clears when temperature normalizes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans run, coils are clean, and door seals are good but temperature stays high, compressor or refrigerant needs inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet fault — insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: Fisher & Paykel washers are known for their innovative top-load designs. E4 indicates a water supply issue preventing proper filling.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screen
fixes.description: Remove hose and clean mesh filter at washer port.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel authorized service available for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump fault — drainage problem
error_codes.detail: The drain pump is not functioning correctly. Water is not draining from the drum.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the filter access point on your model. Drain water and clean.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Verify routing and no kinks.

fixes.title: Replace pump
fixes.description: FP pumps are quality but do fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel has an extensive authorized service network.

error_codes.meaning: Lid switch fault — lid not detected as closed (top-load models)
error_codes.detail: The lid switch is not detecting the lid as closed. The washer will not agitate or spin for safety.

fixes.title: Inspect switch actuator
fixes.description: Check the plastic tab that triggers the lid switch.

fixes.title: Test lid switch
fixes.description: Multimeter test for continuity when depressed.

fixes.title: Replace lid switch
fixes.description: Standard repair on top-load models.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, close lid and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If switch replacement doesn't resolve E6, wiring harness to the switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Motor controller fault
error_codes.detail: The motor controller has detected a fault in motor operation. Fisher & Paykel uses a brushless motor system that is generally very reliable.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes.

fixes.title: Check for overloading
fixes.description: Remove half the load and retry.

fixes.title: Contact F&P service
fixes.description: Motor controller faults typically need authorized service diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E2 returns, authorized service required.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel motor systems are proprietary — authorized service is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Not draining — drain fault
error_codes.detail: Fisher & Paykel's DishDrawer dishwashers use a unique drawer design. U4 indicates the unit cannot drain water within the expected time.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean the filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the filter in the bottom of the drawer.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm proper routing with high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear, pump may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel DishDrawer repair requires model-specific parts.

error_codes.meaning: Flood protection activated — water in base
error_codes.detail: The flood protection system has detected water in the base tray of the dishwasher.

fixes.title: Tilt to drain base
fixes.description: Tilt unit forward to drain base tray (power off).
fixes.warning: Turn off power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for damage.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect all internal connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, dry. Code clears when sensor resets.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no visible leak, internal crack needs F&P authorized inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Heating failure — water not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The DishDrawer's heating system has failed. Water is not heating to the programmed temperature.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor
fixes.description: At room temp: ~6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install appropriate replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating component replacement requires careful waterproof resealing.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: The Haier washer is not detecting water entering the drum during the fill cycle.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold valves must be fully open.

fixes.title: Check inlet hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to machine.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity.

fixes.title: Replace valve if failed
fixes.description: Install new valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good, inlet valve replacement resolves this.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Haier washer cannot drain water within the allowed time.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access via lower front panel. Drain and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper high-loop installation.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Door not closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The washer cannot detect a properly closed and locked door.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing caught in the gasket.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push firmly until definite click.

fixes.title: Replace door lock
fixes.description: If latch or solenoid is worn.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but E4 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor error — drive motor fault
error_codes.detail: The washing machine motor is not functioning correctly.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items — overloading can trigger motor errors.

fixes.title: Test motor
fixes.description: Access motor and test winding continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes, reduce load, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor control board or motor replacement requires professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Water level sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The water level pressure sensor is not reading correctly.

fixes.title: Check pressure hose
fixes.description: Locate the small hose connecting the tub to the pressure sensor. Ensure it's not kinked, cracked, or disconnected.

fixes.title: Test pressure sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect hose from sensor and gently blow — should hear a click from the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixes.description: If sensor doesn't respond to air pressure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pressure hose and sensor are fine, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor failure — freezer compartment
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor in the freezer has failed. Temperature regulation may be compromised.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: At 32°F/0°C: approximately 16k ohms. At room temp: approximately 6k–8k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat thermistor connector.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Clip old sensor off evaporator coil and install new one.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, allow 24 hours temperature stabilization.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The defrost sensor has failed. Defrost cycles may not complete correctly, leading to frost buildup.

fixes.title: Manually defrost
fixes.description: Unplug and allow complete defrost before diagnosing.

fixes.title: Test sensor
fixes.description: Should read 5k–16k ohms depending on temperature.

fixes.title: Replace sensor
fixes.description: Unclip from evaporator and install new sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours for normal operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If defrost sensor is new and correct, defrost heater or control board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — control board fault
error_codes.detail: The main control board has lost communication with a sub-module or display board.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check all connectors
fixes.description: Inspect and reseat all wiring connectors at the control board.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If power cycle and connections don't resolve it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F6 returns, control board replacement needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If board replacement doesn't resolve F6, wiring harness between boards has failed.
